NKJV.1: Oh, give thanks to the LORD, for He is good! For His mercy endures forever.
NKJV.2: Let Israel now say, “His mercy endures forever.”
NKJV.3: Let the house of Aaron now say, “His mercy endures forever.”
NKJV.4: Let those who fear the LORD now say, “His mercy endures forever.”
NKJV.5: I called on the LORD in distress; The LORD answered me and set me in a broad place.
NKJV.6: The LORD is on my side; I will not fear. What can man do to me?
NKJV.7: The LORD is for me among those who help me; Therefore I shall see my desire on those who hate me.
NKJV.8: It is better to trust in the LORD Than to put confidence in man.
NKJV.9: It is better to trust in the LORD Than to put confidence in princes.
NKJV.10: All nations surrounded me, But in the name of the LORD I will destroy them.
NKJV.11: They surrounded me, Yes, they surrounded me; But in the name of the LORD I will destroy them.
NKJV.12: They surrounded me like bees; They were quenched like a fire of thorns; For in the name of the LORD I will destroy them.
NKJV.13: You pushed me violently, that I might fall, But the LORD helped me.
NKJV.14: The LORD is my strength and song, And He has become my salvation.
NKJV.15: The voice of rejoicing and salvation Is in the tents of the righteous; The right hand of the LORD does valiantly.
NKJV.16: The right hand of the LORD is exalted; The right hand of the LORD does valiantly.
NKJV.17: I shall not die, but live, And declare the works of the LORD.
NKJV.18: The LORD has chastened me severely, But He has not given me over to death.
NKJV.19: Open to me the gates of righteousness; I will go through them, And I will praise the LORD.
NKJV.20: This is the gate of the LORD,  Through which the righteous shall enter.
NKJV.21: I will praise You, For You have answered me, And have become my salvation.
NKJV.22: The stone which the builders rejected Has become the chief cornerstone.
NKJV.23: This was the LORD’s doing; It is marvelous in our eyes.
NKJV.24: This is the day the LORD has made; We will rejoice and be glad in it.
NKJV.25: Save now, I pray, O LORD; O LORD, I pray, send now prosperity.
NKJV.26: Blessed is he who comes in the name of the LORD! We have blessed you from the house of the LORD.
NKJV.27: God is the LORD, And He has given us light; Bind the sacrifice with cords to the horns of the altar.
NKJV.28: You are my God, and I will praise You; You are my God, I will exalt You.
NKJV.29: Oh, give thanks to the LORD, for He is good! For His mercy endures forever.

ESV.1: Oh give thanks to the LORD, for he is good; for his steadfast love endures forever!
ESV.2: Let Israel say, “His steadfast love endures forever.”
ESV.3: Let the house of Aaron say, “His steadfast love endures forever.”
ESV.4: Let those who fear the LORD say, “His steadfast love endures forever.”
ESV.5: Out of my distress I called on the LORD; the LORD answered me and set me free.
ESV.6: The LORD is on my side; I will not fear. What can man do to me?
ESV.7: The LORD is on my side as my helper; I shall look in triumph on those who hate me.
ESV.8: It is better to take refuge in the LORD than to trust in man.
ESV.9: It is better to take refuge in the LORD than to trust in princes.
ESV.10: All nations surrounded me; in the name of the LORD I cut them off!
ESV.11: They surrounded me, surrounded me on every side; in the name of the LORD I cut them off!
ESV.12: They surrounded me like bees; they went out like a fire among thorns; in the name of the LORD I cut them off!
ESV.13: I was pushed hard,  so that I was falling, but the LORD helped me.
ESV.14: The LORD is my strength and my song; he has become my salvation.
ESV.15: Glad songs of salvation are in the tents of the righteous: “The right hand of the LORD does valiantly,
ESV.16: the right hand of the LORD exalts, the right hand of the LORD does valiantly!”
ESV.17: I shall not die, but I shall live, and recount the deeds of the LORD.
ESV.18: The LORD has disciplined me severely, but he has not given me over to death.
ESV.19: Open to me the gates of righteousness, that I may enter through them and give thanks to the LORD.
ESV.20: This is the gate of the LORD; the righteous shall enter through it.
ESV.21: I thank you that you have answered me and have become my salvation.
ESV.22: The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone.
ESV.23: This is the LORD's doing; it is marvelous in our eyes.
ESV.24: This is the day that the LORD has made; let us rejoice and be glad in it.
ESV.25: Save us, we pray, O LORD! O LORD, we pray, give us success!
ESV.26: Blessed is he who comes in the name of the LORD! We bless you from the house of the LORD.
ESV.27: The LORD is God, and he has made his light to shine upon us. Bind the festal sacrifice with cords, up to the horns of the altar!
ESV.28: You are my God, and I will give thanks to you; you are my God; I will extol you.
ESV.29: Oh give thanks to the LORD, for he is good; for his steadfast love endures forever!

NASB.1: Give thanks to the LORD, for He is good; For His lovingkindness is everlasting.
NASB.2: Oh let Israel say, 'His lovingkindness is everlasting.'
NASB.3: Oh let the house of Aaron say, 'His lovingkindness is everlasting.'
NASB.4: Oh let those who fear the LORD say, 'His lovingkindness is everlasting.'
NASB.5: From [my] distress I called upon the LORD; The LORD answered me [and] [set me] in a large place.
NASB.6: The LORD is for me; I will not fear; What can man do to me?
NASB.7: The LORD is for me among those who help me; Therefore I will look [with satisfaction] on those who hate me.
NASB.8: It is better to take refuge in the LORD Than to trust in man.
NASB.9: It is better to take refuge in the LORD Than to trust in princes.
NASB.10: All nations surrounded me; In the name of the LORD I will surely cut them off.
NASB.11: They surrounded me, yes, they surrounded me; In the name of the LORD I will surely cut them off.
NASB.12: They surrounded me like bees; They were extinguished as a fire of thorns; In the name of the LORD I will surely cut them off.
NASB.13: You pushed me violently so that I was falling, But the LORD helped me.
NASB.14: The LORD is my strength and song, And He has become my salvation.
NASB.15: The sound of joyful shouting and salvation is in the tents of the righteous; The right hand of the LORD does valiantly.
NASB.16: The right hand of the LORD is exalted; The right hand of the LORD does valiantly.
NASB.17: I will not die, but live, And tell of the works of the LORD.
NASB.18: The LORD has disciplined me severely, But He has not given me over to death.
NASB.19: Open to me the gates of righteousness; I shall enter through them, I shall give thanks to the LORD.
NASB.20: This is the gate of the LORD; The righteous will enter through it.
NASB.21: I shall give thanks to You, for You have answered me, And You have become my salvation.
NASB.22: The stone which the builders rejected Has become the chief corner [stone].
NASB.23: This is the LORD'S doing; It is marvelous in our eyes.
NASB.24: This is the day which the LORD has made; Let us rejoice and be glad in it.
NASB.25: O LORD, do save, we beseech You; O LORD, we beseech You, do send prosperity!
NASB.26: Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the LORD; We have blessed you from the house of the LORD.
NASB.27: The LORD is God, and He has given us light; Bind the festival sacrifice with cords to the horns of the altar.
NASB.28: You are my God, and I give thanks to You; [You are] my God, I extol You.
NASB.29: Give thanks to the LORD, for He is good; For His lovingkindness is everlasting.

NLT.1:
Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good!
His faithful love endures forever.
NLT.2:
Let all Israel repeat:
“His faithful love endures forever.”
NLT.3:
Let Aaron’s descendants, the priests, repeat:
“His faithful love endures forever.”
NLT.4:
Let all who fear the LORD repeat:
“His faithful love endures forever.”
NLT.5:
In my distress I prayed to the LORD,
and the LORD answered me and set me free.
NLT.6:
The LORD is for me, so I will have no fear.
What can mere people do to me?
NLT.7:
Yes, the LORD is for me; he will help me.
I will look in triumph at those who hate me.
NLT.8:
It is better to take refuge in the LORD
than to trust in people.
NLT.9:
It is better to take refuge in the LORD
than to trust in princes.
NLT.10:
Though hostile nations surrounded me,
I destroyed them all with the authority of the LORD.
NLT.11:
Yes, they surrounded and attacked me,
but I destroyed them all with the authority of the LORD.
NLT.12:
They swarmed around me like bees;
they blazed against me like a crackling fire.
But I destroyed them all with the authority of the LORD.
NLT.13:
My enemies did their best to kill me,
but the LORD rescued me.
NLT.14:
The LORD is my strength and my song;
he has given me victory.
NLT.15:
Songs of joy and victory are sung in the camp of the godly.
The strong right arm of the LORD has done glorious things!
NLT.16:
The strong right arm of the LORD is raised in triumph.
The strong right arm of the LORD has done glorious things!
NLT.17:
I will not die; instead, I will live
to tell what the LORD has done.
NLT.18:
The LORD has punished me severely,
but he did not let me die.
NLT.19:
Open for me the gates where the righteous enter,
and I will go in and thank the LORD.
NLT.20:
These gates lead to the presence of the LORD,
and the godly enter there.
NLT.21:
I thank you for answering my prayer
and giving me victory!
NLT.22:
The stone that the builders rejected
has now become the cornerstone.
NLT.23:
This is the LORD’s doing,
and it is wonderful to see.
NLT.24:
This is the day the LORD has made.
We will rejoice and be glad in it.
NLT.25:
Please, LORD, please save us.
Please, LORD, please give us success.
NLT.26:
Bless the one who comes in the name of the LORD.
We bless you from the house of the LORD.
NLT.27:
The LORD is God, shining upon us.
Take the sacrifice and bind it with cords on the altar.
NLT.28:
You are my God, and I will praise you!
You are my God, and I will exalt you!
NLT.29:
Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good!
His faithful love endures forever.

WEB.1: Give thanks to Yahweh, for he is good, for his loving kindness endures forever.
WEB.2: Let Israel now say that his loving kindness endures forever.
WEB.3: Let the house of Aaron now say that his loving kindness endures forever.
WEB.4: Now let those who fear Yahweh say that his loving kindness endures forever.
WEB.5: Out of my distress, I called on Yah. Yah answered me with freedom.
WEB.6: Yahweh is on my side. I will not be afraid. What can man do to me?
WEB.7: Yahweh is on my side among those who help me. Therefore I will look in triumph at those who hate me.
WEB.8: It is better to take refuge in Yahweh, than to put confidence in man.
WEB.9: It is better to take refuge in Yahweh, than to put confidence in princes.
WEB.10: All the nations surrounded me, but in Yahweh’s name, I cut them off.
WEB.11: They surrounded me, yes, they surrounded me. In Yahweh’s name I indeed cut them off.
WEB.12: They surrounded me like bees. They are quenched like the burning thorns. In Yahweh’s name I cut them off.
WEB.13: You pushed me back hard, to make me fall, but Yahweh helped me.
WEB.14: Yah is my strength and song. He has become my salvation.
WEB.15: The voice of rejoicing and salvation is in the tents of the righteous. “The right hand of Yahweh does valiantly.
WEB.16: The right hand of Yahweh is exalted! The right hand of Yahweh does valiantly!”
WEB.17: I will not die, but live, and declare Yah’s works.
WEB.18: Yah has punished me severely, but he has not given me over to death.
WEB.19: Open to me the gates of righteousness. I will enter into them. I will give thanks to Yah.
WEB.20: This is the gate of Yahweh; the righteous will enter into it.
WEB.21: I will give thanks to you, for you have answered me, and have become my salvation.
WEB.22: The stone which the builders rejected has become the cornerstone.
WEB.23: This is Yahweh’s doing. It is marvelous in our eyes.
WEB.24: This is the day that Yahweh has made. We will rejoice and be glad in it!
WEB.25: Save us now, we beg you, Yahweh! Yahweh, we beg you, send prosperity now.
WEB.26: Blessed is he who comes in Yahweh’s name! We have blessed you out of Yahweh’s house.
WEB.27: Yahweh is God, and he has given us light. Bind the sacrifice with cords, even to the horns of the altar.
WEB.28: You are my God, and I will give thanks to you. You are my God, I will exalt you.
WEB.29: Oh give thanks to Yahweh, for he is good, for his loving kindness endures forever.

AMP.1:
O give thanks to the LORD, for He is good;
For His lovingkindness endures forever.
AMP.2:
Oh let Israel say,
"His lovingkindness endures forever."
AMP.3:
Oh let the house of Aaron say,
"His lovingkindness endures forever."
AMP.4:
Oh let those who [reverently] fear the LORD, say,
"His lovingkindness endures forever."
AMP.5:
Out of my distress I called on the LORD;
The LORD answered me and set me free.
AMP.6:
The LORD is on my side; I will not fear.
What can [mere] man do to me?
AMP.7:
The LORD is on my side, He is among those who help me;
Therefore I will look [in triumph] on those who hate me.
AMP.8:
It is better to take refuge in the LORD
Than to trust in man.
AMP.9:
It is better to take refuge in the LORD
Than to trust in princes.
AMP.10:
All nations encompassed me;
In the name of the LORD I will surely cut them off.
AMP.11:
They encompassed me, yes, they surrounded me [on every side];
In the name of the LORD I will cut them off.
AMP.12:
They swarmed around me like bees;
They flare up and are extinguished like a fire of thorns;
In the name of the LORD I will surely cut them off.
AMP.13:
You [my enemy] pushed me violently so that I was falling,
But the LORD helped me.
AMP.14:
The LORD is my strength and song,
And He has become my salvation.
AMP.15:
The sound of joyful shouting and salvation is in the tents of the righteous:
The right hand of the LORD does valiantly.
AMP.16:
The right hand of the LORD is exalted;
The right hand of the LORD does valiantly.
AMP.17:
I will not die, but live,
And declare the works and recount the illustrious acts of the LORD.
AMP.18:
The LORD has disciplined me severely,
But He has not given me over to death.
AMP.19:
Open to me the [temple] gates of righteousness;
I shall enter through them, I shall give thanks to the LORD.
AMP.20:
This is the gate of the LORD;
The righteous will enter through it.
AMP.21:
I will give thanks to You, for You have heard and answered me;
And You have become my salvation [my Rescuer, my Savior].
AMP.22:
The stone which the builders rejected
Has become the chief corner stone.
AMP.23:
This is from the LORD and is His doing;
It is marvelous in our eyes.
AMP.24:
This [day in which God has saved me] is the day which the LORD has made;
Let us rejoice and be glad in it.
AMP.25:
O LORD, save now, we beseech You;
O LORD, we beseech You, send now prosperity and give us success!
AMP.26:
Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the LORD;
We have blessed you from the house of the LORD [you who come into His sanctuary under His guardianship].
AMP.27:
The LORD is God, and He has given us light [illuminating us with His grace and freedom and joy].
Bind the festival sacrifices with cords to the horns of the altar.
AMP.28:
You are my God, and I give thanks to You;
[You are] my God, I extol You.
AMP.29:
O give thanks to the LORD, for He is good;
For His lovingkindness endures forever.

MSG.1:
Thank God because he's good,
because his love never quits.
MSG.2:
Tell the world, Israel,
"His love never quits."
MSG.3:
And you, clan of Aaron, tell the world,
"His love never quits."
MSG.4:
And you who fear God, join in,
"His love never quits."
MSG.5:
Pushed to the wall, I called to God;
from the wide open spaces, he answered.
MSG.6:
God's now at my side and I'm not afraid;
who would dare lay a hand on me?
MSG.7:
God's my strong champion;
I flick off my enemies like flies.
MSG.8:
Far better to take refuge in God
than trust in people;
MSG.9:
Far better to take refuge in God
than trust in celebrities.
MSG.10:
Hemmed in by barbarians,
in God's name I rubbed their faces in the dirt;
MSG.11:
Hemmed in and with no way out,
in God's name I rubbed their faces in the dirt;
MSG.12:
Like swarming bees, like wild prairie fire, they hemmed me in;
in God's name I rubbed their faces in the dirt.
MSG.13:
I was right on the cliff-edge, ready to fall,
when God grabbed and held me.
MSG.14:
God's my strength, he's also my song,
and now he's my salvation.
MSG.15:
Hear the shouts, hear the triumph songs
in the camp of the saved?
"The hand of God has turned the tide!
MSG.16:
The hand of God is raised in victory!
The hand of God has turned the tide!"
MSG.17:
I didn't die. I lived!
And now I'm telling the world what God did.
MSG.18:
God tested me, he pushed me hard,
but he didn't hand me over to Death.
MSG.19:
Swing wide the city gates—the righteous gates!
I'll walk right through and thank God!
MSG.20:
This Temple Gate belongs to God,
so the victors can enter and praise.
MSG.21:
Thank you for responding to me;
you've truly become my salvation!
MSG.22:
The stone the masons discarded as flawed
is now the capstone!
MSG.23:
This is God's work.
We rub our eyes—we can hardly believe it!
MSG.24:
This is the very day God acted—
let's celebrate and be festive!
MSG.25:
Salvation now, God. Salvation now!
Oh yes, God—a free and full life!
MSG.26:
Blessed are you who enter in God's name—
from God's house we bless you!
MSG.27:
God is God,
he has bathed us in light.
Festoon the shrine with garlands,
hang colored banners above the altar!
MSG.28:
You're my God, and I thank you.
O my God, I lift high your praise.
MSG.29:
Thank God—he's so good.
His love never quits!

NIV.1:
Psalm 118
Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good;
his love endures forever.
NIV.2:
Let Israel say:
“His love endures forever.”
NIV.3:
Let the house of Aaron say:
“His love endures forever.”
NIV.4:
Let those who fear the Lord say:
“His love endures forever.”
NIV.5:
In my anguish I cried to the Lord,
and he answered by setting me free.
NIV.6:
The Lord is with me; I will not be afraid.
What can man do to me?
NIV.7:
The Lord is with me; he is my helper.
I will look in triumph on my enemies.
NIV.8:
It is better to take refuge in the Lord
than to trust in man.
NIV.9:
It is better to take refuge in the Lord
than to trust in princes.
NIV.10:
All the nations surrounded me,
but in the name of the Lord I cut them off.
NIV.11:
They surrounded me on every side,
but in the name of the Lord I cut them off.
NIV.12:
They swarmed around me like bees,
but they died out as quickly as burning thorns;
in the name of the Lord I cut them off.
NIV.13:
I was pushed back and about to fall,
but the Lord helped me.
NIV.14:
The Lord is my strength and my song;
he has become my salvation.
NIV.15:
Shouts of joy and victory
resound in the tents of the righteous:
“The Lord’s right hand has done mighty things!
NIV.16:
The Lord’s right hand is lifted high;
the Lord’s right hand has done mighty things!”
NIV.17:
I will not die but live,
and will proclaim what the Lord has done.
NIV.18:
The Lord has chastened me severely,
but he has not given me over to death.
NIV.19:
Open for me the gates of righteousness;
I will enter and give thanks to the Lord.
NIV.20:
This is the gate of the Lord
through which the righteous may enter.
NIV.21:
I will give you thanks, for you answered me;
you have become my salvation.
NIV.22:
The stone the builders rejected
has become the capstone;
NIV.23:
the Lord has done this,
and it is marvelous in our eyes.
NIV.24:
This is the day the Lord has made;
let us rejoice and be glad in it.
NIV.25:
O Lord, save us;
O Lord, grant us success.
NIV.26:
Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord.
From the house of the Lord we bless you.
NIV.27:
The Lord is God,
and he has made his light shine upon us.
With boughs in hand, join in the festal procession
up to the horns of the altar.
NIV.28:
You are my God, and I will give you thanks;
you are my God, and I will exalt you.
NIV.29:
Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good;
his love endures forever.

NRSVCE.1:
O give thanks to the LORD, for he is good;
his steadfast love endures forever!
NRSVCE.2:
Let Israel say,
“His steadfast love endures forever.”
NRSVCE.3:
Let the house of Aaron say,
“His steadfast love endures forever.”
NRSVCE.4:
Let those who fear the LORD say,
“His steadfast love endures forever.”
NRSVCE.5:
Out of my distress I called on the LORD;
the LORD answered me and set me in a broad place.
NRSVCE.6:
With the LORD on my side I do not fear.
What can mortals do to me?
NRSVCE.7:
The LORD is on my side to help me;
I shall look in triumph on those who hate me.
NRSVCE.8:
It is better to take refuge in the LORD
than to put confidence in mortals.
NRSVCE.9:
It is better to take refuge in the LORD
than to put confidence in princes.
NRSVCE.10:
All nations surrounded me;
in the name of the LORD I cut them off!
NRSVCE.11:
They surrounded me, surrounded me on every side;
in the name of the LORD I cut them off!
NRSVCE.12:
They surrounded me like bees;
they blazed like a fire of thorns;
in the name of the LORD I cut them off!
NRSVCE.13:
I was pushed hard, so that I was falling,
but the LORD helped me.
NRSVCE.14:
The LORD is my strength and my might;
he has become my salvation.
NRSVCE.15:
There are glad songs of victory in the tents of the righteous:
“The right hand of the LORD does valiantly;
NRSVCE.16:
the right hand of the LORD is exalted;
the right hand of the LORD does valiantly.”
NRSVCE.17:
I shall not die, but I shall live,
and recount the deeds of the LORD.
NRSVCE.18:
The LORD has punished me severely,
but he did not give me over to death.
NRSVCE.19:
Open to me the gates of righteousness,
that I may enter through them
and give thanks to the LORD.
NRSVCE.20:
This is the gate of the LORD;
the righteous shall enter through it.
NRSVCE.21:
I thank you that you have answered me
and have become my salvation.
NRSVCE.22:
The stone that the builders rejected
has become the chief cornerstone.
NRSVCE.23:
This is the LORD’s doing;
it is marvelous in our eyes.
NRSVCE.24:
This is the day that the LORD has made;
let us rejoice and be glad in it.
NRSVCE.25:
Save us, we beseech you, O LORD!
O LORD, we beseech you, give us success!
NRSVCE.26:
Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the LORD.
We bless you from the house of the LORD.
NRSVCE.27:
The LORD is God,
and he has given us light.
Bind the festal procession with branches,
up to the horns of the altar.
NRSVCE.28:
You are my God, and I will give thanks to you;
you are my God, I will extol you.
NRSVCE.29:
O give thanks to the LORD, for he is good,
for his steadfast love endures forever.

NET.1: Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good and his loyal love endures!
NET.2: Let Israel say, “Yes, his loyal love endures!”
NET.3: Let the family of Aaron say, “Yes, his loyal love endures!”
NET.4: Let the loyal followers of the LORD say, “Yes, his loyal love endures!”
NET.5: In my distress I cried out to the LORD. The LORD answered me and put me in a wide open place.
NET.6: The LORD is on my side, I am not afraid! What can people do to me?
NET.7: The LORD is on my side as my helper. I look in triumph on those who hate me.
NET.8: It is better to take shelter in the LORD than to trust in people.
NET.9: It is better to take shelter in the LORD than to trust in princes.
NET.10: All the nations surrounded me. Indeed, in the name of the LORD I pushed them away.
NET.11: They surrounded me, yes, they surrounded me. Indeed, in the name of the LORD I pushed them away.
NET.12: They surrounded me like bees. But they disappeared as quickly as a fire among thorns. Indeed, in the name of the LORD I pushed them away.
NET.13: “You aggressively attacked me and tried to knock me down, but the LORD helped me.
NET.14: The LORD gives me strength and protects me; he has become my deliverer.”
NET.15: They celebrate deliverance in the tents of the godly. The LORD’s right hand conquers,
NET.16: the LORD’s right hand gives victory, the LORD’s right hand conquers.
NET.17: I will not die, but live, and I will proclaim what the LORD has done.
NET.18: The LORD severely punished me, but he did not hand me over to death.
NET.19: Open for me the gates of the just king’s temple! I will enter through them and give thanks to the LORD.
NET.20: This is the LORD’s gate — the godly enter through it.
NET.21: I will give you thanks, for you answered me, and have become my deliverer.
NET.22: The stone which the builders discarded has become the cornerstone.
NET.23: This is the LORD’s work. We consider it amazing!
NET.24: This is the day the LORD has brought about. We will be happy and rejoice in it.
NET.25: Please LORD, deliver! Please LORD, grant us success!
NET.26: May the one who comes in the name of the LORD be blessed! We will pronounce blessings on you in the LORD’s temple.
NET.27: The LORD is God and he has delivered us. Tie the offering with ropes to the horns of the altar!
NET.28: You are my God and I will give you thanks! You are my God and I will praise you!
NET.29: Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good and his loyal love endures!
